edition: CDC Field Triage Guidelines for Injured Patients (current edition)

steps.name: Vitals and consciousness

criteria.label: GCS <= 13
SBP < 110 mmHg adult, or pediatric shock index abnormal
RR < 10 or > 29, or respiratory distress / need for ventilatory support

steps.action: If any criterion: transport to highest-level trauma center.

steps.name: Anatomy of injury

criteria.label: Penetrating injuries to head, neck, torso, or proximal extremities
Amputation proximal to wrist or ankle
Suspected pelvic fracture
Two or more proximal long-bone fractures
Crushed, degloved, mangled, or pulseless extremity
Chest wall instability or deformity (flail chest)
Open or depressed skull fracture
Paralysis or suspected spinal cord injury

steps.action: If any criterion: transport to highest-level trauma center.

steps.name: Mechanism of injury

criteria.label: Falls: adult > 20 ft, pediatric > 10 ft or 2-3x height
High-risk auto crash: intrusion > 12 in occupant site / > 18 in any site, ejection, death same compartment, telemetry consistent with high risk
Auto vs pedestrian/bicyclist thrown, run over, or impact > 20 mph
Motorcycle crash > 20 mph

steps.action: Consider transport to a trauma center; agency-protocol decision.

steps.name: Special considerations

criteria.label: Older adult: SBP < 110 may indicate shock; low-impact mechanisms still high risk
Children should be triaged preferentially to pediatric-capable centers
On anticoagulant or with bleeding disorder
Pregnancy > 20 weeks
Burns: with or without trauma; trauma centers for combined

steps.action: Use clinical judgment; consult medical control.
